context: Contract section-1-renderer-core--section-3-output-and-errors open_questions_for_builders asked: handle empty/whitespace input inside renderer (return ok:true, no DOM changes) vs short-circuit in consumer.
decision: Renderer short-circuits empty/whitespace input itself: returns {ok:true} and clears target's children. Consumer code is also free to short-circuit and not call render at all; both paths satisfy S5.A2/A3.
rationale: Defensive: lets the renderer be safe to call with any string without producing a KaTeX error for an empty input.
nested_equivalent: In a nested-dispatch run, the section-1 builder would ask the section-1 Overseer for a ruling on the open contract question; Overseer would pick this branch because it is strictly additive and matches the 'empty in, empty out' principle stated as the consumer's expected behavior in S5.A2.
